steps.title: Use substitution for the repeated expression
steps.content:
The term $(4-8x)$ appears on both sides of the equation. Let $u = 4-8x$ to simplify. The equation becomes:
$$18 - 4u = 5 - 5u$$

steps.title: Solve for u
steps.content:
Collect $u$ terms on the left and constants on the right:
$$18 - 4u + 5u = 5$$
$$18 + u = 5$$
$$u = -13$$
Since $u = 4-8x$, the value of $4-8x$ is $-13$.
